Tree is overhanging parking lot from the shored-up wall area behind the building. Falling Fruit season: August-October.
Also several other species of trees here. Looks like either Aspen or Birch through most the stretch, and a type of red oak towards the western end of the strip of trees.
9050 N Church Dr, Parma Heights, OH 44130, USA
Overhanging small stream and sidewalk, very tasty.
A shrubby apple tree NW of ridge and roycroft intersection, standing alone. Falling Fruit season: September-October.
Some haws and crabapple on southern edge of woods.

Weeping and black willows mixed with crabapples, some cherry, grape, raspberries. Mock strawberry (safe to eat but tasteless) is under black willow branch that fell over towards field, along with Holly closer to the trunk. Beware of poison ivy.
Eating sized apple here, just in treeline south of large trench crossing field. All apples gone when visited in early august - early season apple. Falling Fruit season: June-July.
Eating-sized apples, currently unripe as of 7/6/16. Mixed into bushy edge on way into park. Falling Fruit season: September-November.

Huge, extensive greenbrier patches North of parking lot/ west of pond. A good amount of witch-hazel mixed in with it and mostly under mature white oak canopy. Maples are more prevalent on the west side of woods and black cherries (mostly small) are scattered t
Tree at NW corner of green area surrounded by pavement. Heavy fruit as of 2015. Falling Fruit season: September-October.
All located in this strip of green behind eye doctor's office.
Spotted a few hickory trees near this edge of the woodline, there was a witch-hazel nearby. Oaks (various types) and beech trees scattered throughout.
Along the back fence of parking lot. Blooming in May.
